pregnancy.reason: No amount of alcohol is known to be safe during pregnancy; it crosses the placenta directly.

nursing.reason: Passes into breast milk; wait until fully metabolized (roughly 2 hours per standard drink) before nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Generally limited to under 200mg/day (about one 12oz coffee); higher intake is linked to increased miscarriage/low birth weight risk.

nursing.reason: Small amounts pass into milk; moderate intake is usually fine but watch for infant jitteriness or poor sleep.

pregnancy.reason: Can stimulate uterine contractions at higher doses; tonic water amounts are low but caution is still advised.

nursing.reason: Passes into milk in small amounts; occasional tonic water is generally considered low-risk.

pregnancy.reason: Considered safe in moderation by most regulators, except for those with phenylketonuria (PKU).

nursing.reason: Same PKU caveat applies; otherwise considered low-risk in moderation.

pregnancy.reason: Crosses the placenta and clears slowly from fetal tissue; most guidelines suggest moderation or avoidance.

nursing.reason: Passes into breast milk and is cleared slowly by infants; best used sparingly.

pregnancy.reason: Limited safety data in pregnancy; moderation advised.

nursing.reason: Limited safety data during nursing; moderation advised.

pregnancy.reason: Approved by regulators but crosses the placenta; moderate intake recommended.

nursing.reason: Passes into breast milk in small amounts; moderate intake recommended.

pregnancy.reason: Not metabolized by the body and considered safe in moderation during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Minimal transfer into breast milk; considered safe in moderation.

pregnancy.reason: Considered safe in typical food amounts; no established link to adverse pregnancy outcomes.

nursing.reason: Considered safe in typical food amounts while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: High intake of glycyrrhizin has been linked to preterm birth and effects on fetal development.

nursing.reason: Can reduce milk supply and passes into milk in small amounts; best limited.

pregnancy.reason: Unpasteurized dairy can carry Listeria, which poses a serious risk to the fetus.

nursing.reason: Listeria risk applies to the nursing parent's health regardless of feeding status.

pregnancy.reason: Safe if made with pasteurized milk; unpasteurized soft cheeses carry a Listeria risk.

nursing.reason: No specific nursing risk once the cheese itself is confirmed pasteurized.

pregnancy.reason: Risk of Listeria plus very high vitamin A content, both of concern in pregnancy.

nursing.reason: No specific nursing restriction; eat in moderation as with any liver product.

pregnancy.reason: Listeria risk unless heated until steaming hot before eating.

nursing.reason: No specific nursing restriction beyond normal food safety.

pregnancy.reason: Raw sprouts carry a notable risk of Salmonella and E. coli contamination.

nursing.reason: No specific nursing restriction beyond normal food safety.

pregnancy.reason: Risk of parasites/bacteria; choose reputable sources and consider mercury content of the fish species.

nursing.reason: No specific nursing restriction beyond normal food safety and mercury guidance.

pregnancy.reason: One of the highest-mercury fish; mercury accumulates in fetal tissue and can affect brain development.

nursing.reason: High mercury content can transfer into breast milk.

pregnancy.reason: High mercury content poses a risk to fetal neurological development.

nursing.reason: High mercury content can transfer into breast milk.

pregnancy.reason: High mercury content poses a risk to fetal neurological development.

nursing.reason: High mercury content can transfer into breast milk.

pregnancy.reason: High mercury content poses a risk to fetal neurological development.

nursing.reason: High mercury content can transfer into breast milk.

pregnancy.reason: Moderate mercury content; most guidelines suggest limiting to 1-2 servings per week, less for albacore.

nursing.reason: Moderate mercury content; limit servings as with pregnancy guidance.

pregnancy.reason: Risk of Salmonella; choose pasteurized eggs or cook thoroughly.

nursing.reason: No specific nursing restriction beyond normal food safety.

pregnancy.reason: Risk of E. coli or Salmonella contamination without pasteurization.

nursing.reason: Same contamination risk applies, though the concern is for the nursing parent rather than the infant directly.

pregnancy.reason: High-dose preformed vitamin A is linked to birth defects; avoid supplements/products exceeding recommended limits.

nursing.reason: Topical use is generally lower risk than oral supplementation; consult a provider for high-dose products.

pregnancy.reason: A form of vitamin A associated with birth defect risk at high doses; most obstetric guidance recommends avoiding retinoid skincare during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Limited data on topical absorption into milk; many providers advise caution.

pregnancy.reason: Strongly linked to severe birth defects; prescription retinoids are contraindicated in pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Insufficient safety data; typically avoided while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Low-concentration topical use is generally considered acceptable; oral/high-dose salicylates should be avoided.

nursing.reason: Low-concentration topical use is generally considered acceptable; avoid high oral doses.

pregnancy.reason: Systemic absorption is higher than other skin-lightening agents; most guidance recommends avoiding during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Limited safety data on transfer into breast milk.

pregnancy.reason: Minimal systemic absorption through skin; generally considered acceptable for acne treatment.

nursing.reason: Minimal systemic absorption; generally considered acceptable.

pregnancy.reason: A known reproductive toxicant; avoid products releasing formaldehyde (some hair treatments, nail products).

nursing.reason: Avoid exposure; can be absorbed through skin and inhalation.

pregnancy.reason: Linked to developmental toxicity; found in some nail products, best avoided or used with ventilation.

nursing.reason: Inhalation exposure can affect the nursing parent's health; use with caution and ventilation.

pregnancy.reason: Suspected endocrine disruptor with some studies linking exposure to developmental effects; minimize where possible.

nursing.reason: Suspected endocrine disruptor; minimize exposure where possible.

pregnancy.reason: Weak endocrine-disrupting potential suspected; evidence is limited but some choose to minimize exposure.

nursing.reason: Same limited-evidence caution applies while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: A chemical sunscreen filter with some endocrine-disruption concerns; mineral sunscreens are often preferred during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Same endocrine-disruption caution applies while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: A known abortifacient herb; can cause severe toxicity and miscarriage.

nursing.reason: Toxic to infants; never use while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Can stimulate uterine contractions and has been linked to serious cardiovascular effects in newborns when used to induce labor.

nursing.reason: Insufficient safety data; avoid while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Traditionally used to induce menstruation/labor; can stimulate uterine contractions.

nursing.reason: Insufficient safety data; avoid while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: A uterine stimulant traditionally avoided during pregnancy due to miscarriage risk.

nursing.reason: Insufficient safety data; avoid while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Hormonal effects make it unsuitable during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Hormonal effects make it unsuitable while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Generally short-term use appears low-risk, but data is limited; check with a provider.

nursing.reason: Limited safety data; use with provider guidance.

pregnancy.reason: Some animal studies raise concern about fetal effects; limited human data.

nursing.reason: Limited safety data during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Sometimes used near term to encourage labor; avoid earlier in pregnancy without medical guidance.

nursing.reason: Insufficient safety data; avoid while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Interacts with many medications and has limited pregnancy safety data.

nursing.reason: May reduce milk supply and has limited infant safety data.

pregnancy.reason: Can cause dangerous blood pressure changes; unsafe during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Unsafe stimulant effects; avoid while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Linked to liver toxicity and sedation; unsafe during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Sedative effects and liver toxicity risk make it unsafe while nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids that are toxic to the liver and potentially the fetus.

nursing.reason: Liver-toxic compounds can transfer into breast milk.

pregnancy.reason: Occasional short-term use for constipation is generally considered acceptable; avoid prolonged use.

nursing.reason: Considered compatible with nursing in typical laxative doses.

pregnancy.reason: Oral aloe latex is a strong laxative that can stimulate uterine contractions.

nursing.reason: Can pass into milk and cause infant GI upset.

pregnancy.reason: High supplemental doses (beyond prenatal vitamin levels) aren't well studied in pregnancy.

nursing.reason: High supplemental doses aren't well studied during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: High-dose supplementation beyond prenatal levels isn't well studied and offers no proven benefit.

nursing.reason: High-dose supplementation isn't well studied during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Commonly recommended during pregnancy to prevent anemia; follow prenatal dosing guidance.

nursing.reason: Commonly recommended and considered safe during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Recommended before and during pregnancy to reduce neural tube defect risk.

nursing.reason: Recommended and considered safe during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Supports fetal brain development; commonly recommended in moderate doses.

nursing.reason: Supports infant brain development via breast milk; commonly recommended.

pregnancy.reason: Generally considered safe and may support digestive health during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Generally considered safe during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: A sugar alcohol generally considered safe in food amounts during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Generally considered safe in food amounts during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Generally safe but can cause digestive upset in larger amounts.

nursing.reason: Generally safe but can cause digestive upset in larger amounts.

pregnancy.reason: A widely used preservative considered safe (GRAS) in typical food amounts.

nursing.reason: Considered safe in typical food amounts during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: A widely used preservative considered safe (GRAS) in typical food amounts.

nursing.reason: Considered safe in typical food amounts during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: Some evidence of GI irritation at high intake; food-grade amounts are generally considered low risk.

nursing.reason: Same low-level GI concern applies during nursing.

pregnancy.reason: A common food fat with no specific pregnancy safety concern beyond general nutrition.

nursing.reason: No specific nursing safety concern.

pregnancy.reason: A common food emulsifier with no known pregnancy safety concern.

nursing.reason: No known nursing safety concern.

pregnancy.reason: Found in cured/processed meats; moderate intake is typically fine, but combine with the deli-meat heating advice above.

nursing.reason: Moderate intake in processed meats is typically considered acceptable.

pregnancy.reason: Synthetic antioxidants with some animal-study concerns; typical food amounts are considered low risk by regulators.

nursing.reason: Typical food amounts are considered low risk by regulators.

pregnancy.reason: An artificial dye linked to hyperactivity concerns in some studies; not specifically hazardous in pregnancy but often minimized.

nursing.reason: Same general caution as other artificial dyes.

pregnancy.reason: Insufficient safety data on fetal development; major health bodies recommend avoiding during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Can transfer into breast milk; insufficient infant safety data.

pregnancy.reason: Linked to effects on fetal brain development; recommended to avoid entirely during pregnancy.

nursing.reason: Transfers into breast milk and can affect infant development.

pregnancy.reason: A well-established cause of pregnancy complications and developmental harm.

nursing.reason: Transfers into breast milk and can affect infant sleep and health.

pregnancy.reason: Generally avoided, especially in the third trimester, due to risks to fetal circulation; consult a provider.

nursing.reason: Considered compatible with nursing at standard doses; minimal transfer into milk.
